conditions.name: Ehlers-Danlos Syndromes
conditions.description: A group of inherited connective-tissue disorders that affect collagen, the protein that gives structure to joints, skin, blood vessels, and organs. Features vary widely between the subtypes.
signals: Joints that hyperextend or partially dislocate (sublux)
Skin that bruises, stretches, or scars unusually
Chronic joint and muscle pain
Often co-occurs with POTS, MCAS, or GI issues

conditions.name: Hypermobility Spectrum Disorders
conditions.description: Symptomatic joint hypermobility that doesn’t meet the criteria for a specific EDS subtype. Symptoms and management overlap closely with hypermobile EDS.
signals: Joints that move beyond the normal range
Pain and instability across multiple joints
Recurrent sprains, strains, or subluxations
Fatigue and frequent soft-tissue injuries

conditions.name: POTS / Dysautonomia
conditions.description: Dysfunction of the autonomic nervous system, which controls heart rate, blood pressure, and digestion. In POTS, the heart rate rises sharply on standing.
signals: Lightheadedness or fainting when upright
Rapid heart rate on standing
Fatigue, brain fog, and exercise intolerance
Frequently overlaps with EDS and MCAS

conditions.name: Mast Cell Disease
conditions.description: A condition in which mast cells release chemical mediators (like histamine) inappropriately, producing allergy-like symptoms across multiple body systems.
signals: Flushing, hives, or itching
Abdominal pain, nausea, or food reactions
Headaches and fatigue
Symptoms triggered by foods, stress, or temperature

conditions.name: Chronic Pain & Fibromyalgia
conditions.description: Persistent, widespread pain that often involves changes in how the nervous system processes pain signals (central sensitization), as seen in fibromyalgia.
signals: Widespread muscle and joint pain
Unrefreshing sleep and fatigue
Heightened sensitivity to touch, light, or sound
Brain fog and difficulty concentrating

conditions.name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome
conditions.description: Severe, persistent pain, usually in a limb, that is out of proportion to any original injury, often with changes in skin color, temperature, and swelling.
signals: Burning or throbbing pain in a limb
Skin color or temperature changes
Swelling and sensitivity to light touch
Pain disproportionate to the triggering event

conditions.name: Spinal Arachnoiditis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the arachnoid membrane surrounding the spinal cord and nerves, which can cause chronic pain and neurological symptoms.
signals: Burning or stinging back and leg pain
Numbness, tingling, or weakness
Symptoms that worsen with activity
Bladder or bowel changes in some cases

conditions.name: Craniocervical Instability
conditions.description: Excessive movement between the skull and upper spine, which can occur in connective-tissue disorders and irritate the brainstem and nearby nerves.
signals: Headache at the base of the skull
A feeling the head is too heavy for the neck
Dizziness, visual, or swallowing changes
Symptoms that worsen when upright

conditions.name: Chronic Inflammation
conditions.description: Low-grade, ongoing immune activation that can contribute to pain, fatigue, and other systemic symptoms in connective-tissue conditions.
signals: Persistent fatigue and malaise
Diffuse aches and stiffness
Flares triggered by stress or illness
Often overlaps with MCAS and autoimmune issues

conditions.name: Back Pain
conditions.description: In hypermobile patients, back pain often stems from joint instability, disc issues, or muscle compensation rather than a single structural cause.
signals: Aching or instability in the low or mid back
Pain with prolonged sitting or standing
Muscle spasms and fatigue
Improvement with targeted stability work
